methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Primoco UAV is a publicly traded defense and security company founded by Ladislav Semetkovský, operating within a sector comprising 24 peers total: 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. The company has raised €44M+ with an active orderbook.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage peer coverage. The Defense & Security sector lacks three or more same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier characteristics, rendering a filtered peer-median approach unreliable. An all-sector median would introduce material bias given the sector's heterogeneity in business model, customer base, and technology maturity. The identified public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operate at different scale and market positioning than Primoco's current footprint.

Without triangulation across sufficient peer multiples, revenue or EBITDA multiples cannot be reliably anchored. A complete valuation would require alternative methods—such as precedent transaction analysis within defense unmanned systems, discounted cash flow modeling tied to orderbook conversion and program revenue visibility, or strategic buyer premium analysis—none of which are supported by the available data. Current evidence supports only the observation that Primoco operates in a capital-light, order-backed mode with €44M+ in committed revenue, but lacks sufficient comps-based support for a point estimate or range.
